title: Trigonometry
summary: 9709 P1 trigonometry — radians, the unit circle, exact values, and graphs of sin, cos, and tan with GeoGebra unit circle explorer.
learningObjectives: Use the unit circle definitions of sin θ and cos θ.
Recall exact values at standard angles.
Sketch and use graphs of trigonometric functions.

simpleExplanation.title: Circles and triangles
simpleExplanation.summary: Trigonometry links angles to ratios in triangles and to coordinates on the unit circle. Radians are the natural unit for calculus later in the course.
steps: On the unit circle, cos θ = x-coordinate, sin θ = y-coordinate.
Convert: π rad = 180° — use fractions of π for standard angles.
Memorise exact values at 0, π/6, π/4, π/3, π/2.
Graphs of sin and cos have period 2π; tan has period π with asymptotes.

sections.content: **Trigonometry on P1** tests exact values, graph shapes, and simple equations. The unit circle is the bridge to **P3 identities** — build it correctly now.
Arc length s = rθ (θ in radians) | Sector area = ½r²θ | sin²θ + cos²θ = 1

items: ASTC diagram for signs in each quadrant
Solve sin x = k by finding reference angle + quadrants
Always check solutions in the given interval

sections.question: Solve sin θ = ½ for 0 ≤ θ ≤ 2π.
sections.solution:
Reference angle: sin⁻¹(½) = π/6.

Sin is positive in Q1 and Q2.

θ = **π/6** or θ = **5π/6**.

sections.content: Cambridge often sets trig equations on **0 ≤ x ≤ 2π** or **0° ≤ x ≤ 360°**. List **all** solutions in the interval — missing the second root loses A marks.
